sections.paragraph:
const. שֵׁשֶׁת m. six (often occurring). (A numeral, which is widely extended even beyond the Phœnicio-Shemitic languages; Arab. سِتُّ, سِتَّةُ Aram. שֵׁת which see; Eth. ስሲ፡ Sanscr. shash, Zend. qswas, Slav. schest, Gr. ἕξ, Lat. sex, to which every one can easily add the forms used in modern languages.)-Plur. שִׁשִּׁים sixty (often occurring).

Derivatives, שִׁשָּׁה, שִׁשִּׁי.

II.
